issue_summary: Deprecate Predicate in favor of java.util.function.Predicate
javadoc_before:
/** * Defines a functor interface implemented by classes that perform a predicate * test on an object. *

* A {@code Predicate} is the object equivalent of an {@code if} statement. * It uses the input object to return a true or false value, and is often used in * validation or filtering. *

*

* Standard implementations of common predicates are provided by * {@link PredicateUtils}. These include true, false, instanceof, equals, and, * or, not, method invocation and null testing. *

* * @param the type that the predicate queries * * @since 1.0 */
javadoc_after:
/** * Defines a functor interface implemented by classes that perform a predicate * test on an object. *

* A {@code Predicate} is the object equivalent of an {@code if} statement. * It uses the input object to return a true or false value, and is often used in * validation or filtering. *

*

* Standard implementations of common predicates are provided by * {@link PredicateUtils}. These include true, false, instanceof, equals, and, * or, not, method invocation and null testing. *

* * @param the type that the predicate queries * * @since 1.0 * @deprecated Use {@link java.util.function.Predicate}. */
